description:
You are assisting an MLOps engineer who needs to consolidate the results of several model–training runs.  
A small sandbox workspace already exists on the machine:

    /home/user/experiments/
        ├── exp1/
        │   ├── log.txt
        │   └── metrics.csv
        ├── exp2/
        │   ├── log.txt
        │   └── metrics.csv
        └── exp3/
            ├── log.txt
            └── metrics.csv

What each file contains (structure only, the actual numbers vary per experiment):

1. log.txt – a free-form training log (~15–20 lines) that *always* ends with two lines that look exactly like  
        Final accuracy: <float>  
        Final loss: <float>  
   There is exactly one space after each colon, and the float is printed with two decimal places.

2. metrics.csv – a comma-separated file with a header line  
        epoch,accuracy,loss  
   followed by one row per epoch, e.g.  
        1,0.72,0.85  

Your tasks

A. Create a new directory  
       /home/user/experiment_summary  
   (create any missing parent directories as needed).

B. Inside that directory generate a tab-separated summary file named  
       summary.tsv  
   with *four* lines total:
       1. A header line that is literally  
              Experiment<TAB>Accuracy<TAB>Loss  
       2-4. One line per experiment directory (exp1, exp2, exp3) in **lexicographic order**.  
            • Column 1 → the directory name (e.g. exp1)  
            • Column 2 → the floating-point value from “Final accuracy:” (copied verbatim)  
            • Column 3 → the floating-point value from “Final loss:”   (copied verbatim)  

   Example of the required layout (values are just placeholders – your script must extract the real ones):
       Experiment\tAccuracy\tLoss\n
       exp1\t0.83\t0.61\n
       exp2\t0.88\t0.52\n
       exp3\t0.79\t0.73\n

   The file *must* end with a single trailing newline.

C. Still inside /home/user/experiment_summary create another file called  
       high_loss.log  
   containing the names of every experiment whose final loss is **strictly greater than 0.60**.  
   • One experiment name per line, no extra whitespace.  
   • The file must also end with a single trailing newline.  
   • If no experiment qualifies, create an empty file (size 0 bytes).

Restrictions

1. Use only standard GNU/Linux userland tools that ship with a typical Ubuntu/Debian installation.  
2. At least one awk command and one sed command must appear somewhere in your solution workflow (the grading script merely checks the final files, but this constraint simulates a realistic interview requirement).  
3. You do NOT have sudo privileges; work entirely under /home/user.  

Verification details the grader will look for

1. /home/user/experiment_summary exists and is a directory.  
2. /home/user/experiment_summary/summary.tsv exists, is regular, and its content exactly matches the specification above (correct header, correct order, correct tab separation, correct numeric values, exactly one trailing newline).  
3. /home/user/experiment_summary/high_loss.log exists, is regular, and lists the correct experiment names (loss > 0.60 rule) or is empty when appropriate, again with exactly one trailing newline when non-empty.  

No other output is required. Good luck!

/home/user/experiment_summary/summary.tsv

exp3\t0.79\t0.73\n
-------------------------------------------------

(note: each “\n” represents a single newline byte; there is exactly one newline after the last line)

/home/user/experiment_summary/high_loss.log
-------------------------------------------------
exp1\n
exp3\n
-------------------------------------------------

Both files must have Unix line endings (LF only). Permissions may remain default (0644 for files, 0755 for directories).
